overview: The witest28-7 application is a minimal Hello World web application composed of a single frontend component served as a static site via a lightweight Go HTTP server. The server handles root and 404 routing, injects HTTP security headers, and exposes a /health endpoint for liveness checks. There are no backend APIs or databases — all content is rendered server-side as semantic HTML with inline CSS.
requirements: The root URL (/) must return HTTP 200 and render a page displaying 'Hello, World!' as the primary heading (h1).
The page <title> tag must read 'Hello World' (or equivalent meaningful text).
The page must be fully visible without scrolling on screen widths as small as 320px and on standard desktop sizes.
The application must meet WCAG 2.1 Level AA accessibility standards, including a minimum text contrast ratio of 4.5:1.
The page must use semantic HTML markup (e.g., <main>, <h1>) readable by screen readers.
The application must render correctly in the latest two major versions of Chrome, Firefox, Safari, and Edge.
The page must fully load within 2 seconds on a ≥10 Mbps connection and total page weight must not exceed 500 KB.
The application must achieve a Google Lighthouse performance score of 90 or above.
The application must be served over HTTPS at a stable, publicly accessible URL.
The application must include HTTP security headers: Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy, and Permissions-Policy.
The application must not expose server-side error details or stack traces to end users.
Requests to non-existent routes must return HTTP 404 with a user-friendly page that includes a link back to the main page.
A /health endpoint must return HTTP 200 to support liveness/readiness probes.
The application must target 99% monthly uptime.

components.name: hello-world-web
components.openAPISpec:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Hello World Web
  description: Minimal Hello World web application serving HTML pages.
  version: 1.0.0
paths:
  /:
    get:
      summary: Hello World homepage
      description: Returns the Hello World HTML page with HTTP 200.
      responses:
        '200':
          description: HTML page displaying Hello World greeting.
          content:
            text/html:
              schema:
                type: string
  /health:
    get:
      summary: Health check
      description: Liveness and readiness probe endpoint.
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Service is healthy.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  status:
                    type: string
                    example: ok
  /{path}:
    get:
      summary: Catch-all 404 handler
      description: Returns a user-friendly 404 page for any unrecognised route.
      parameters:
        - name: path
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      responses:
        '404':
          description: Page not found. Returns HTML with a link back to the homepage.
          content:
            text/html:
              schema:
                type: string
components.componentAgentInstructions:
## Component: hello-world-web ### Purpose A lightweight Go HTTP server that serves a Hello World HTML page, a 404 error page, and a /health endpoint. There is no database, no external API calls, and no JavaScript dependencies. ### Language & Framework - **Language:** Go 1.22+ - **HTTP Library:** standard library `net/http` only — no third-party frameworks. - **Port:** `9090` ### Directory Layout ``` /hello-world-web main.go Dockerfile go.mod ``` ### main.go Implementation 1. **Security headers middleware** — wrap every handler with a middleware function that sets the following headers on every response before writing: - `Content-Security-Policy: default-src 'self'; style-src 'unsafe-inline'` - `X-Frame-Options: DENY` - `X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff` - `Referrer-Policy: no-referrer` - `Permissions-Policy: geolocation=(), microphone=(), camera=()` 2. **Route: GET /** — return HTTP 200, `Content-Type: text/html; charset=utf-8`, and the following HTML (inline, no external assets): ```html Hello World

Hello, World!

``` - Text color `#1a1a1a` on white `#ffffff` provides a contrast ratio well above 4.5:1 (WCAG AA). - The `clamp` font size ensures visibility on screens 320px wide without scrolling. - No external fonts, scripts, or images — keeps total page weight well under 500 KB. 3. **Route: GET /health** — return HTTP 200, `Content-Type: application/json`, body `{"status":"ok"}`. 4. **Catch-all handler** — for any other path, return HTTP 404, `Content-Type: text/html; charset=utf-8`, and the following HTML: ```html Page Not Found – Hello World

404 – Page Not Found

The page you are looking for does not exist.

Go back to the homepage
``` - Link color `#0057b8` on white meets WCAG AA contrast. 5. **Do NOT serve any other method** — for non-GET requests to `/` or `/health`, return HTTP 405 Method Not Allowed. 6. **No stack traces exposed** — use `log.Println` for internal errors; never write Go error strings into the HTTP response body. 7. **Startup** — listen on `0.0.0.0:9090`. Log `"server listening on :9090"` at startup. ### go.mod ``` module github.com/witest28-7/hello-world-web go 1.22 ``` (No external dependencies.) ### Dockerfile ```dockerfile FROM golang:1.22-alpine AS builder WORKDIR /app COPY go.mod . COPY main.go . RUN go build -o server . FROM alpine:3.19 RUN addgroup -S app && adduser -S app -G app USER app WORKDIR /app COPY --from=builder /app/server . EXPOSE 9090 CMD ["./server"] ``` ### Edge Cases - All paths except `/` and `/health` must fall through to the 404 handler — use `http.ServeMux` and register exact patterns for `/` (exact) using a trailing-slash-less match plus a separate catch-all, or compare `r.URL.Path` inside the `/` handler and return 404 HTML if path != "/". - Ensure the security headers middleware wraps all handlers including /health. - The server must handle concurrent requests safely — `net/http` default server is sufficient; no shared mutable state.
